steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, a calculator table or graph can confirm the forms match the original function. The form that displays the minimum value as a constant is B, $f(x)=(x-4)^{2}+(-256)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Use vertex form
steps.content: For an upward-opening parabola written as $f(x)=a(x-h)^2+k$, the minimum value is the constant $k$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The minimum value is shown as the constant $-256$ in vertex form. Choice B — $f(x)=(x-4)^{2}+(-256)$, $(x-4)^2+(-256)$, shows that value, so B is correct.
